items.definition_en: Wind is air that moves from one place to another.
items.example_en: The wind was strong and blew my hat away.

items.definition_en: A breeze is a gentle and cool wind.
items.example_en: We enjoyed the evening breeze by the lake.

items.definition_en: A gust is a sudden, strong burst of wind.
items.example_en: A gust knocked over the empty chair outside.

items.definition_en: A gale is a very strong wind.
items.example_en: The gale broke tree branches in the park.

items.definition_en: A draft is cold air that comes into a room.
items.example_en: I felt a draft near the window when it opened.

items.definition_en: A windstorm is a storm with very strong winds.
items.example_en: Last night a windstorm knocked out the power.

items.definition_en: A whirlwind is a strong, spinning wind.
items.example_en: A small whirlwind lifted leaves into the air.

items.definition_en: Airflow is how air moves from one place to another.
items.example_en: Open windows improve the airflow in the house.

items.definition_en: A breeze block is something that blocks the wind.
items.example_en: They put a breeze block wall in the garden.

items.definition_en: A windmill is a building with blades turned by the wind.
items.example_en: The old windmill stands on top of the hill.

items.definition_en: A tailwind is a wind that blows behind something moving.
items.example_en: Cyclists go faster when they have a tailwind.

items.definition_en: A headwind is a wind that blows against you as you move.
items.example_en: It is harder to run against a headwind.

items.definition_en: Windchill is the cold you feel because of the wind.
items.example_en: The windchill made it feel much colder outside.

items.definition_en: A twister is a strong, turning wind that moves in a circle.
items.example_en: The town was hit by a twister last year.

items.definition_en: An air current is a flow of moving air.
items.example_en: Birds can glide on warm air currents.

items.definition_en: A breeze way connects two buildings and lets wind pass through.
items.example_en: We walked through the cool breeze way between the houses.

items.definition_en: A windbreak is something that protects from the wind.
items.example_en: Farmers plant trees as a windbreak for crops.

items.definition_en: A storm is weather with strong wind and rain.
items.example_en: We stayed inside during the storm.

items.definition_en: A tempest is a violent storm with strong wind.
items.example_en: The ship was caught in a wild tempest at sea.

items.definition_en: A wind vane shows the direction the wind is blowing.
items.example_en: A metal wind vane spun on the barn roof.
